notes: sun rises and sets, returns — cyclic futility

notes: wind goes south, turns north — cyclical

notes: rivers run to sea, sea not full

notes: all things wearisome; eye not satisfied with seeing

notes: famous: nothing new under the sun

label: despair
notes: already been in ages before us

notes: no remembrance of former, none of latter

notes: I the Preacher was king over Israel

notes: applied heart to seek wisdom — sore travail given by God

notes: famous: all vanity and vexation of spirit

notes: crooked cannot be made straight

notes: I have come to great estate, increased in wisdom

notes: to know wisdom, madness, folly — also vexation of spirit

notes: famous: in much wisdom is much grief; he who increases knowledge increases sorrow

label: despair
notes: tested with mirth, behold also vanity

label: despair
notes: laughter is mad, what does pleasure accomplish

notes: give heart to wine, lay hold on folly to see what was good

notes: made great works, built houses, planted vineyards

notes: made gardens and orchards, planted trees

notes: made pools to water forest of trees

notes: got servants, herds, flocks above all before me

notes: gathered silver, gold, peculiar treasure of kings

label: narrative
notes: became great, increased above all before me; wisdom remained

label: narrative
notes: whatever eyes desired, I withheld not — heart rejoiced in labor

notes: all vanity and vexation of spirit, no profit under sun

notes: turned to behold wisdom and folly

label: instruction
notes: wisdom excels folly as light excels darkness

label: despair
notes: wise has eyes; fool walks in darkness — but one event happens to both

notes: why am I more wise — also vanity

label: despair
notes: no remembrance of wise more than fool — wise dies as fool

notes: hated life because work grievous; all vanity

notes: hated all my labor I leave to him after me

notes: who knows if heir will be wise or fool — also vanity

notes: caused heart to despair of all labor

label: despair
notes: work in wisdom, leave to one who has not labored — vanity and great evil

notes: what has man of all his labor and vexation

notes: all his days sorrows; heart not rest in night

notes: famous carpe diem refrain: nothing better than to eat, drink, enjoy good of labor — gift from God

notes: who can eat or have enjoyment more than I

notes: to good man God gives wisdom; to sinner travail; also vanity

notes: famous: to every thing there is a season

notes: time to be born, die; plant, pluck up

notes: time to kill, heal; break down, build up

notes: time to rend, sew; silence, speak

notes: time to love, hate; war, peace

notes: refrain — what profit has worker

notes: travail God has given sons of men

notes: famous: made everything beautiful in his time; set eternity in heart

notes: no good in them but to rejoice and do good

notes: every man should eat, drink, enjoy labor — gift of God

notes: whatever God does shall be forever; men should fear before him

notes: that which has been is now; God requires what is past

notes: saw under sun place of judgment, wickedness was there

notes: God shall judge righteous and wicked

notes: all go to one place, all of dust, all turn to dust

notes: who knows spirit of man goes upward, beast downward

notes: oppressions under sun; tears of oppressed, no comforter

notes: praised dead more than living

notes: better than both is he not yet been, has not seen evil work

notes: every right work man is envied of neighbor — vanity

notes: fool folds hands, eats own flesh

notes: better handful with quietness than two with travail

notes: returned, saw vanity under sun

notes: one alone, no second; never satisfied — sore travail

notes: two together, they have heat — one alone how warm

notes: famous: threefold cord not quickly broken

notes: be not rash with mouth; God in heaven, let words be few

notes: when vow vow to God, defer not to pay

notes: profit of earth for all; king served by field

notes: famous: lover of silver not satisfied with silver — also vanity

notes: goods increase, eaters increase; what good to owners

notes: sleep of laborer is sweet; abundance of rich not let him sleep

notes: sore evil saw under sun — riches kept to hurt of owners

notes: riches perish by evil travail; nothing left in hand for sons

notes: as came forth naked, return — nothing of his labor takes away

notes: sore evil — what profit labored for wind

notes: all days eats in darkness, much sorrow, sickness

notes: famous carpe diem: good for one to eat, drink, enjoy good of labor — his portion

notes: every man God gives riches to enjoy them — gift of God

notes: shall not much remember days of life; God answers in joy of heart

notes: evil seen under sun, common among men

notes: man God gives riches but no power to eat — stranger eats; vanity, evil disease

notes: if begets hundred children, lives many years — but soul not filled — untimely birth better

notes: comes vain, departs in darkness, name covered with darkness

notes: untimely birth has not seen sun — yet has more rest than this man

notes: if lives 1000 years twice, no good — all go to one place

notes: all labor of man for mouth, yet appetite not filled

notes: what has wise more than fool? What poor before living?

notes: better sight of eyes than wandering desire — also vanity, vexation

notes: that which has been is named already; man cannot contend with mightier

notes: many things increase vanity — what is man better

notes: who knows what is good for man in days of vain life — passes as shadow

notes: good name better than precious ointment; day of death than day of birth

notes: better go to house of mourning than feasting — end of all men

notes: better hear rebuke of wise than song of fools

notes: better end of thing than beginning; patient in spirit better than proud

notes: in day of prosperity be joyful; in adversity consider

notes: be not righteous overmuch nor over-wise — why destroy thyself

notes: wisdom strengthens wise more than ten mighty men

notes: take no heed to all words spoken — lest hear servant curse thee

notes: that which is far off and exceeding deep — who can find

notes: behold this I have found, said the preacher, counting one by one

notes: keep king's commandment in regard of oath of God

notes: be not hasty to go from his presence; do not stand in evil thing

notes: king's word has power; who say what doest thou

notes: every purpose has time and judgment; misery great upon him

notes: knows not what shall be; who can tell when shall be

notes: no man has power over spirit nor day of death — wickedness not deliver

notes: saw all this; man rules over another to his own hurt

notes: saw wicked buried, came in remembrance — also vanity

notes: sinner does evil hundred times; yet I know it shall be well with those that fear God

notes: vanity on earth — just men receive according to wicked work; wicked according to just

notes: famous: commended mirth — nothing better than to eat, drink, be merry

notes: applied heart to know wisdom; saw business done — sees no sleep day or night

notes: man cannot find out work of God under sun

notes: righteous and wise in hand of God; man knows not love or hatred

notes: all things alike — one event to righteous and wicked; good and evil

notes: evil among all under sun — heart of sons full of evil; madness; then to dead

notes: famous: there is hope for living; better living dog than dead lion

notes: living know they shall die; dead know nothing

notes: famous carpe diem: go thy way, eat bread with joy, drink wine with merry heart — God accepts works

notes: let garments always be white; let head lack no ointment

notes: famous: race not to swift, battle not to strong — time and chance happen to all

notes: man knows not his time; like fish, birds — sons of men snared in evil time

notes: this wisdom seen under sun — seemed great to me

notes: little city, few men; great king came, besieged it

notes: poor wise man delivered city by wisdom; no man remembered him

notes: wisdom better than strength; wisdom of poor despised, words not heard

notes: words of wise heard in quiet more than cry of him who rules among fools

notes: wisdom better than weapons — one sinner destroys much good

notes: wise heart at right hand; fool's at left

notes: folly set in great dignity; rich sit in low place

notes: famous: remember now thy Creator in days of youth

notes: doors shut in streets; sound of grinding low; rises at voice of bird

notes: silver cord loosed, golden bowl broken — death imagery

notes: refrain: vanity of vanities, all is vanity

notes: of making many books no end; much study weariness of flesh
